query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "reproducing, analyzing, and detecting reward hacking in rubric-based r" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on general exploration mechanisms in reinforcement learning (specifically value bonuses and ensemble errors) and does not address the specific domain of "reward hacking," "rubric-based reward modeling," or the detection of such behaviors in LLMs, which are the core constructs of the user's question. While both fields fall under the broad umbrella of RL, the paper lacks the specific mechanism (reward hacking detection) or the specific domain (rubric-based LLM evaluation) require
